institutionName: North Carolina Agricultural and Technical State University
collegeName: College of Agriculture and Environmental Sciences (CAES)
programName: Bachelor of Science in Biological Engineering — Bioprocess
tagline: Engineer Life — From Biomolecules to Biofuels
programDescription: The Bioprocess concentration in Biological Engineering applies engineering principles to biological systems for applications in biopharmaceuticals, biofuels, food processing, and environmental remediation. Students design and optimize bioprocesses including fermentation, purification, and scale-up. The program is ABET-accredited, ensuring graduates meet rigorous engineering standards for professional practice.
accreditationBody: ABET (Accreditation Board for Engineering and Technology)

careerOutlook.outlook: Bioprocess engineers are at the forefront of the bioeconomy, designing sustainable production systems for pharmaceuticals, biofuels, and biomaterials. The $400 billion global biotech market continues to drive demand for ABET-trained engineers.
